steps.name: Drawing out steel, forging the curved sabre blade and full tang
Forging the knucklebow and guard assembly
Normalizing the blade
Rough grinding the bevels and fullers
Final grind, polishing, and sharpening the edge
Fitting the guard, wrapping the tang
Forge welding and drawing out steel, forging blade blank and tang
Forging the knucklebow guard from remaining steel
Normalizing the blade
Rough grinding and establishing the edge geometry
Applying a thick layer of clay to the spine of the blade, leaving the edge exposed
Drying clay
Heating the blade and quenching in water
Letting cool.  Scraping off baked clay and grinding away scale to inspect the hardened edge
Final sanding and polishing
Carving wooden handle.  Wrapping handle.
Forge welding and drawing out steel, forging blade blank and tang
Forging the knucklebow guard from remaining steel
Normalizing the blade
Rough grinding and establishing the edge geometry
Heating and thorough hardening blade
Carefully grinding forge scale to observe temper colors
Tempering
Final sanding and polishing
Carving wooden handle.  Wrapping handle.  Hack n' slash!

//: this makes the simple makeshift glaive, but changing the id would break e.g.  tilesets.

steps.name: Drawing out steel, forging the blade blank, and drawing out the tang
Forging the belly and hammering in the bevels
Normalizing the blade to relieve forging stress
Rough grinding the bevels to establish the heavy chopping geometry
Filing the traditional notch at the base of the blade to arrest stress cracks
Final sharpening, sanding, and polishing
Carving the wooden handle, burning the hidden tang into the wood, and securing it
Drawing out medium carbon steel, forging the blade blank, and drawing out the hidden tang
Forging an exaggerated forward belly to counteract thermal shock bending
Normalizing the blade
Rough grinding and filing the notch
Heating the entire blade evenly to critical temperature
Differentially hardening by carefully pouring water over the cutting edge, using the thermal shock to pull the blade into shape.  Quickly hammering out imperfections
Waiting for spine to air cool slightly before quenching
Final sharpening, sanding, and polishing
Carving the wooden handle, burning the tang into the wood and assembling
Drawing out medium carbon steel, forging the blade blank, and drawing out the hidden tang
Forging an exaggerated forward belly to counteract thermal shock bending
Normalizing the blade
Rough grinding and filing the notch
Heating the entire blade evenly to critical temperature
Thorough hardening
Grinding away forge scale to observe temper colors
Tempering
Final sharpening, sanding, and polishing
Carving the wooden handle, burning the tang into the wood and assembling

//: basically 2.5x the resources of a single machete to cover the fact that it's two weapons, each with a hand guard

steps.name: Forge welding and cutting steel
Drawing out steel, forging the blade blank, and drawing out the hidden tang
Normalizing the blade
Rough grinding and filing the fuller
Heating the entire blade evenly to critical temperature
Differentially hardening by edge-quenching in water.  Pulling the blade from the water while the thick spine still retains heat.
Quickly grinding the edge scale to observe colors.  Allowing residual heat from the spine to temper the edge to a straw color
Final sharpening, sanding, and polishing
Wrapping tang
Forge welding and cutting steel
Drawing out steel, forging the blade blank, and drawing out the hidden threaded tang.  Using remaining steel to forge pommel
Normalizing the blade
Rough grinding and filing the fuller
Heating the entire blade to critical temperature
Thorough hardening
Quickly grinding the edge scale to observe temper colors
Tempering
Final sharpening, sanding, and polishing
Wrapping tang and threading pommel
Forge welding and cutting steel

Heating and differentially hardening blade
Final sharpening, sanding, and polishing
Wrapping tang and threading pommel

Quickly grinding the edge scale to observe temper colors
Tempering

Wrapping tang and threading pommel
Cutting steel.  Forging the blade to shape and drawing out the tang
Normalizing blade
Truing the profile and rough grinding the bevels
Applying refractory clay to the spine for differential hardening
Letting clay dry
Heating to critical temperature and water quenching
Letting spine bleed heat into the blade.  Stripping off clay and quenching fully
Final grinding
Carving and fitting the handle components

Truing the profile and rough grinding the bevels
Heating to critical temperature and thorough hardening
Tempering

Cutting steel.  Forging the blade straight and drawing out the tang
Normalizing blade
Truing the profile and rough grinding the bevels
Applying clay to spine
Letting clay dry
Heating to critical temperature and water quenching
Letting spine bleed heat into the blade.  Stripping off clay and quenching fully
Final grinding.  Establishing razor-sharp edge
Carving handle and assembling
Cutting steel.  Forging the blade to shape and drawing out the tang

Truing the profile and rough grinding the bevels
Heating to critical temperature and thorough hardening
Tempering

Carving handle.  Fitting the handle components.  Masaka, a masterpiece!

Rough grinding

Tempering
